content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

November 18, 1993


Roy C. Abrams
City Attorney
City of San Mateo
330 West Twentieth Avenue
San Mateo, CA 94403

Re: Your Request for Assistance
Our File No. I-93-394

Dear Mr. Abrams:

This is in response to your request for advice on behalf of
newly-appointed City Councilmember Gary Yates under the conflict-
of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/ Since you
are not seeking advice concerning a specific decision before
Mr. Yates at this time, we are treating your letter as a request
for informal assistance.2/

QUESTION

Does the "public generally" exception permit Councilmember
Yates to participate in a decision to develop 40 acres of property
at Bay Meadows Racetrack which is located within 300 feet of his
residence?

CONCLUSION

So long as 14 percent of the home owners in the City of San
Mateo located near the site will be affected in substantially the
same manner that Councilmember Yates will be affected, the "public


1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections 18000-18954. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Advice File No. I-93-394
Page 2


generally" exception applies. In that case, Councilmember Yates
would be permitted to participate in the decision to develop 40
acres of property at the Bay Meadows Racetrack.

FACTS

Gary Yates is a newly-appointed San Mateo city councilmember
and will take office on December 6, 1993. Councilmember Yates
resides in a single-family residence in a residential development
known as Fiesta Gardens.

Mr. Yates' residence is located within 300 feet of the Bay
Meadows Racetrack. The racetrack is on approximately 130-150
acres of land and consists of a full race track, a 40-acre
practice track, stables, seating, and betting and eating areas.

The 40-acre practice track has been the subject of
development discussions in the last several years. In 1990-91, a
development venture between Prometheus Development Company and the
Jockey Club, the owners of Bay Meadows, was formed. The joint
venture proposed to develop 1300 apartment dwellings on the 40-
acre site. The proposal has been the subject of discussion with
the city council but the proposal was never presented for formal
action. It is anticipated this proposal may be before the city
council in the near future.

There are two neighborhoods which are adjacent to the Bay
Meadows Racetrack. Fiesta Gardens, where Councilmember Yates
resides, is one of the neighborhoods. The other is San Mateo
Glendale Village. The two neighborhoods are exclusively
residential and have 2,638 housing units and a population of
7,914. The total number of housing units in San Mateo is 20,038
and the population is 85,486. These two neighborhoods total 14
percent of owner-occupied units and 9.3 percent of the population.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits public officials from making,
participating in, or using their official position to influence a
governmental decision in which they know, or have reason to know,
they have a financial interest.

An official has a financial interest in a governmental
decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial
effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally,
on the official, or on a member of the official's immediate
family, or on:

Advice File No. I-93-394
Page 3


* * *

(b) Any real property in which the public
official has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

(Section 87103(b).)

Councilmember Yates' personal place of residence is located
within the city of San Mateo and is valued at more than $100,000.
He, thus, has a direct interest in real property and may not make,
participate in making, or in any way attempt to use his official
position to influence a governmental decision, if the decision
will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect,
distinguishable from the effect on the public generally, on his
personal place of residence.

Councilmember Yates' residence is located within 300 feet of
the Bay Meadows Racetrack. Commission Regulation 18702.3 provides
that the effect of a decision is material as to real property in
which an official has a direct interest, if the real property in
which the official has an interest is located within a 300 foot
radius of the boundaries of the property which is the subject of
the decision, unless the decision will have no financial effect
upon the official's real property interest. (Regulation 18702.3.)

However, even when the reasonably foreseeable effect of a
governmental decision is material, disqualification is required
only if the effect of the decision on an official's economic
interest is distinguishable from the effect on the public
generally. (Section 87103.) For the "public generally" exception
to apply, any decision would have to affect a significant segment
of the population of the jurisdiction in substantially the same
manner as it would affect a disqualified public official.

A governmental decision will affect a significant segment of
the public generally if the decision will affect ten percent or
more of all property owners, all home owners, or all households in
the jurisdiction of the official's agency or the district the
official represents. (Regulation 18702.3(a)(1)(A).)

In your letter, you indicated that the two neighborhoods
directly adjacent to the Bay Meadows Racetrack, including the
neighborhood where Councilmember Yates resides, represents 14
percent of all owner-occupied units in the city. Therefore, if
the homes in these neighborhoods will be financially affected in
substantially the same manner, the "public generally" exception

Advice File No. I-93-394
Page 4


will be met and councilmember Yates will have no conflict of
interest. However, we have no facts upon which to make this
determination.

     If we can be of further assistance, please feel free to
contact the Commission's Legal Division at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Steven G. Churchwell
                                        General Counsel /
                                        [signature]

                                        By: Jeanette E. Turvill
                                            Political Reform Consultant
                                            Legal Division

SGC/JET/jt
Enclosures
